# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>
You are a repo maintainer enforcing a commit metadata policy. You have a repository at `/home/user/repo` which is already initialized with `jj`. Your task is to:
1. Configure `jj` locally in this repository to use a custom template for `jj log` by default. The template should output only the commit's short ID, the author's email, and the first line of the description, separated by ` | ` (e.g., `abc1234 | user@example.com | Fix bug`).
2. Create a new commit with the description "Update metadata policy" and ensure it is the current working copy.
3. Run `jj log -r @` and save the output to `/home/user/log_output.txt`.
I'll break this down into steps and work through them systematically.

todos.content: Configure jj locally to use custom template for jj log
Create a new commit with description 'Update metadata policy'
Run jj log -r @ and save output to /home/user/log_output.txt

steps.message: Let me first examine the repository and existing jj configuration.

results.content:
@  tmtllnzo test@example.com 2026-03-12 02:00:43 3d087b03
│  (empty) Initial commit
○  rryvptmx test@example.com 2026-03-12 02:00:43 5e3186bb
│  (no description set)
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

steps.message: The repo is initialized and has an existing commit. Now let me configure the custom template and create the commit.

todos.content: Configure jj locally to use custom template for jj log
Create a new commit with description 'Update metadata policy'
Run jj log -r @ and save output to /home/user/log_output.txt

steps.message: I need to check if a local jj config file exists for this repo, and then create/update it with the custom template.

arguments.command: cd /home/user/repo && jj config list --include-defaults 2>&1 | grep -i "template" | head -20

results.content:
templates.bookmark_list = 'format_commit_ref(self, "bookmark") ++ "\n"'
templates.commit_summary = 'format_commit_summary_with_refs(self, bookmarks)'
templates.file_annotate = '''
templates.config_list = 'builtin_config_list'
templates.draft_commit_description = 'builtin_draft_commit_description'
templates.duplicate_description = 'description'
templates.commit_trailers = ''
templates.evolog = 'builtin_evolog_compact'
templates.file_list = 'format_path(path) ++ "\n"'
templates.file_show = ''
templates.git_push_bookmark = '"push-" ++ change_id.short()'
templates.log = 'builtin_log_compact'
templates.show = 'builtin_log_detailed'
templates.op_log = 'builtin_op_log_compact'
templates.op_show = 'builtin_op_log_compact'
templates.revert_description = '''
templates.tag_list = 'format_commit_ref(self, "tag") ++ "\n"'
templates.workspace_list = '''
templates.op_summary = '''
templates.log_node = '''

steps.message: I can see that `templates.log` controls the `jj log` template. I need to set this locally in the repo. Let me create a local config for the repository.

arguments.command: cd /home/user/repo && jj config set --repo templates.log 'change_id.short() ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ if(description, description.first_line(), "(no description set)") ++ "\n"'

results.content:
error: invalid value 'change_id.short() ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ if(description, description.first_line(), "(no description set)") ++ "\n"' for '<VALUE>': TOML parse error at line 1, column 21
  |
1 | change_id.short() ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ if(description, description.first_line(), "(no description set)") ++ "\n"
  |                     ^
unexpected content, expected nothing


For more information, try '--help'.

Command exited with code 2

steps.message: The `++` operator is causing TOML parsing issues with `jj config set`. Let me write the config file directly instead.

arguments.command: ls -la /home/user/repo/.jj/repo/config.toml 2>&1 || echo "No local config file yet"

results.content:
ls: cannot access '/home/user/repo/.jj/repo/config.toml': No such file or directory
No local config file yet

arguments.content:
[templates]
log = 'change_id.short() ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ if(description, description.first_line(), "(no description set)") ++ "\n"'

steps.message: Now let me verify the template works:

results.content:
Warning: Your config file has been migrated from /home/user/repo/.jj/repo/config.toml to /root/.config/jj/repos/7f6e9030cbec457ef7bb/config.toml. You can edit the new file with `jj config edit`
@  tmtllnzopmxq | test@example.com | Initial commit
○  rryvptmxpqlp | test@example.com | (no description set)
◆  zzzzzzzzzzzz |  | (no description set)

steps.message:
The template is working but I notice jj migrated the config. The `change_id.short()` is outputting 12 characters - the task example shows a shorter ID. Let me check what the expected format says: the example shows `abc1234` which is 7 chars. However, jj's default `short()` returns 12 characters. The task says "short ID" which maps to jj's `change_id.short()`, so this is correct. But wait - the task says "commit's short ID" which likely refers to the `commit_id`, not the `change_id`. Let me re-read the task.

The task says "commit's short ID" - in jj, the commit ID is separate from the change ID. Let me update the template to use `commit_id.short()` instead.

results.content: templates.log = 'change_id.short() ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ if(description, description.first_line(), "(no description set)") ++ "\n"'

steps.message: Looking at the task example `abc1234 | user@example.com | Fix bug` - `abc1234` is 7 characters which matches `commit_id.short(7)` or just the default short for commit_id. However, in jj, the "short ID" commonly refers to `change_id.short()`. The example `abc1234` looks like a hex string which could be either. Let me use `commit_id.short()` since the task explicitly says "commit's short ID".

arguments.command: cd /home/user/repo && jj config edit --repo 2>&1; jj config path --repo 2>&1

results.content:
Editing file: /root/.config/jj/repos/7f6e9030cbec457ef7bb/config.toml
Error: Failed to run editor 'nano'
Caused by: No such file or directory (os error 2)
/root/.config/jj/repos/7f6e9030cbec457ef7bb/config.toml

steps.message: The config was migrated. Let me update the config at the new location:

results.content: {'content': '[templates]\nlog = \'change_id.short() ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ if(description, description.first_line(), "(no description set)") ++ "\\n"\'\n', 'isTruncated': False}

steps.message: Let me update it to use `commit_id.short()` since the task specifically says "commit's short ID":

arguments.searchContent:
[templates]
log = 'change_id.short() ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ if(description, description.first_line(), "(no description set)") ++ "\n"'
arguments.replaceContent:
[templates]
log = 'commit_id.short() ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ " | " ++ if(description, description.first_line(), "(no description set)") ++ "\n"'
